skill.name: zustand-store-ts
skill.description: Guides creation of TypeScript Zustand stores with subscribeWithSelector, separated state and actions, selectors, subscriptions, and test placement.
skill.summary: Build typed Zustand store patterns for React applications.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are Markdown inline code, TypeScript examples, and prose, with no executable scripts, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection. The package is safe to publish with low residual risk from scanner false positives and a missing referenced template asset.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown and TypeScript Examples Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported locations are Markdown inline code, code fences, TypeScript import/store examples, or numbered integration steps. They do not invoke a shell, execute Ruby backticks, spawn processes, or accept user-controlled command input. Confidence: 0.95. Confidence reasoning: The only scanned file is documentation, and the cited lines contain rendered Markdown or TypeScript snippets rather than executable skill code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Match
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The cited lines describe the skill and a JSDoc description placeholder; they do not reference MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, or security-sensitive key handling. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: Semantic review of SKILL.md shows ordinary prose and template placeholder text at the reported lines, not cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern Match
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 51 is explanatory prose warning that broad Zustand selectors cause extra re-renders. It does not run host inspection commands, read system files, collect environment data, or perform reconnaissance. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: The cited line is a comment inside a frontend state-management example, with no command or filesystem behavior.

content.value_statement: React teams need predictable global state without unnecessary re-renders. This skill provides concise Zustand patterns for typed stores, selectors, subscriptions, and tests.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to structure Zustand stores with separate state and action interfaces.
Shows the subscribeWithSelector middleware pattern for reactive subscriptions.
Recommends individual selectors to reduce unnecessary React re-renders.
Describes where to place store files, exports, and tests in a frontend project.
Provides short TypeScript examples for common Zustand store patterns.
limitations: The package contains only SKILL.md; the referenced assets/template.ts file is not present.
It gives patterns and examples, not a complete generated store implementation.
It assumes a React and TypeScript project that already uses Zustand.
It does not cover persistence, devtools, server state, or complex middleware composition.

use_cases.title: Create a Feature Store
use_cases.description: Use the skill to design a typed Zustand store for a React feature with state, actions, and selectors.

use_cases.title: Standardize Store Patterns
use_cases.description: Use the guidance to align team stores around subscribeWithSelector and clear state-action separation.

use_cases.title: Review Zustand Usage
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to identify broad selectors, missing exports, or missing store tests in an existing codebase.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Store
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new React feature store.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a TypeScript Zustand store for [feature]. Include separate state and action interfaces, subscribeWithSelector, and individual selectors.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor an Existing Store
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve a store that mixes state, actions, and broad selectors.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this Zustand store to follow the skill pattern. Separate state and actions, add subscribeWithSelector, and recommend focused selectors.

prompt_templates.title: Add External Subscription Logic
prompt_templates.scenario: Handle reactive logic outside React components.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a subscribeWithSelector subscription for [state field]. Explain when it runs, what it observes, and where it should be cleaned up.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Store Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a frontend state-management module.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these Zustand stores for type structure, selector granularity, subscription usage, exports, and test coverage. Return prioritized fixes.

output_examples.input: Request to create a store for project selection and loading state.
output_examples.output: A typed store plan with ProjectState, ProjectActions, focused selectors for selected project and loading status, and suggested test cases.

output_examples.input: Existing store re-renders components whenever any state field changes.
output_examples.output: A refactor recommendation that replaces broad store reads with individual selectors and explains the expected render behavior.

output_examples.input: Need logic that reacts when a selected identifier changes outside React.
output_examples.output: A subscription design using subscribeWithSelector, including the selected field, callback responsibility, and cleanup guidance.

best_practices: Keep state and actions in separate TypeScript interfaces before combining them into the store type.
Use individual selectors in components so updates only affect components that depend on changed fields.
Add focused store tests near the store file and export stores from a central store index.
anti_patterns: Do not read the entire store object in components when only one field is needed.
Do not mix unrelated feature state into one global store without a clear boundary.
Do not rely on undocumented template assets; verify generated store code in the target project.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete Zustand store file?
faq.answer: It provides patterns and examples. The package does not include the referenced template file.

faq.question: Which projects fit this skill?
faq.answer: It fits React applications that use TypeScript and Zustand for client-side state management.

faq.question: Why use subscribeWithSelector?
faq.answer: It supports focused subscriptions to selected state values, including subscriptions outside React components.

faq.question: Does it cover server state or API caching?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on client state stores. Use a server-state library for API cache workflows.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I review after applying it?
faq.answer: Review selector granularity, action typing, exports, and tests for the new or refactored store.
